rulings.Case Title: VASILIKI SERVETA VS. FINANCE AMERICA, LLC C/O MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 24, 2013, line 1 - DEFENDANT OCWEN LOAN SERVICING, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant OCWEN Loan Servicing LLC's Motion for Summary Judgment is continued to June 6, 2013. Opposing party to provide a full copy of Serveta Deposition Transcript to Department 501 no later than May 28, 2013 4 p.m. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must notify the other party of its intent to appear by 4 p.m. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH MARIE BARNSON KARNAZES VS. ISERVE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 24, 2013, line 9 - DEFENDANT CITIMORTGAGE, INC.'s MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. Defendant Citimortgage, Inc's Motion to Change Venue is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to show that the Complaint as alleged (and prayer for relief) are for "recovery of real property," or "interest therein," or for "determination of interest therein" mandating venue transfer pursuant to CCC 392. =(501/AJR)

Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 24, 2013, line 4 - DEFENDANT TIM CHOY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Choy's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges service of Notice by posting and mailing "because no person of suitable age or discretion can be found there." (Complaint 8(3)(a)). Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that CCP 1161(2) requirements re: rent payment instructions are applicable to other monetary obligations under the Lease; moving party also does not point to any lease provision characterizing "utilities" as "rent". Additionally, moving party fails to address second ground for "3-Day Notice:" rendering Notice sufficient to support unlawful detainer. Richard v. Degen & Brody, Inc. (1960) 181 Cal.App.2d 289, 294 (disapproved on other grounds by Kendal v. Ernest Pestana, Inc. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 488). Defendant's counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must notify the other party of its intent to appear by 4 p.m =(501/AJR)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 24, 2013, line 4 - DEFENDANT TIM CHOY's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant Choy's Motion to Quash Complaint is DENIED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges service of Notice by posting and mailing "because no person of sutiable age or discretion can be found there." (Complaint 8(3)(a)). Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that CCP 1161(2) requirements re: rent payment instructions are applicable to other monetary obligations under the Lease; moving party also does not point to any lease provision characterizing "utilities" as "rent". Additionally, moving party fails to address second ground for "3-Day Notice:" rendering Notice sufficient to support unlawful detainer. Richard v. Degen & Brody, Inc. (1960) 181 Cal.App.2d 289, 294 (disapproved on other grounds by Kendal v. Ernest Pestana, Inc. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 488). Defendant's counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must notify the other party of its intent to appear by 4 p.m. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ZUGER, DINA, TRUSTEE OF THE LEAVENA KHAYAT FAMILY VS. JIMMY AVECEDO et al
